pr.title: Confidence scoring: evidence-mass damping — score = ratio × n/(n+2) (staged option A, BHV-037)
pr.description:
## Summary

Staged design option A (approved after option B / #62): the confidence **score itself** now discounts the success ratio by evidence mass, so the number is honest about how little evidence supports it — one successful run no longer scores 1.0.

In `_compute_score` (shared by the confidence CLI and the promotion loop):

```
base = success_ratio (+ recovery bonus, unchanged)
score = base * n / (n + 2)        # n = execution_count
```

Damped curve (all-pass): 1/1 → 0.3333 `low`, 2/2 → 0.5 `medium`, 5/5 → 0.7143 `high`/ready, 18/18 → 0.9 `very_high`, converging to the raw ratio as n grows. Failures still bite immediately (5/6 → 0.625 drops `high`→`medium` in one application).

**This is a scoring-doctrine change (BHV-037)**: the same factors now yield a lower score than before. Previously persisted confidence reports and committed evidence summaries show scores computed under the old undamped rule and are historical evidence — not recomputed.

Interaction with the #62 single-step ladder (this PR is stacked on it — base is the #62 branch; merge #62 first): the ladder remains as a guard, but on the damped curve an all-pass history already climbs at most one level per run, so `promotion.clamped` is rarely `true` in practice. Belt (damped measurement) and suspenders (rate-limited transitions).

Unchanged: level thresholds (0.9/0.7/0.5/0.3), readiness mapping, recovery-bonus semantics (applied to the ratio before damping), chain/quality/quarantine/duplicate semantics, Revit behavior (2024 baseline unaffected; no live validation required).

Ledger: BHV-037. Skill: `testing-evidence-promotion` checklist items 2 and 13 updated to the damped curve in the same PR.

Tests (tier 2 — targeted): new damping-curve tests in `tests/test_capability_confidence.py` (1/1, 5/5, 18/18, convergence); updated expectations across `tests/test_evidence_promotion.py`, `tests/test_evidence_summary.py`, `tests/test_model_health_evidence.py`, `tests/test_artifact_paths.py`, plus atlas/local-runner/quality/orchestrator suites — 239 passed, 1 skipped; ruff clean. Full pytest runs in CI.

Link to Devin session: https://app.devin.ai/sessions/8ad1c9ac0d2f4252bb77647f1eabaac7
Requested by: @plamen-dev
pr.author: plamen-dev

commits.author: plamen.hristov@imsdevlab.onmicrosoft.com
commits.message:
Promotion: single-step confidence ladder (rate-limit upward level transitions)

One accepted evidence application can no longer jump a capability
very_low -> very_high / blocked -> ready. The published confidence level
rises at most one ladder rung per accepted application; drops are never
clamped. Raw vs effective level + clamped flag are recorded on the
intake record's promotion field. Score doctrine unchanged (still the
pure success ratio). BHV-036.

Co-Authored-By: Devin AI <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commits.author: plamen.hristov@imsdevlab.onmicrosoft.com
commits.message:
Confidence scoring: evidence-mass damping (score = ratio * n/(n+2))

Scoring-doctrine change (BHV-037, staged option A after BHV-036): the
confidence score now discounts the success ratio by evidence mass, so a
single successful run scores ~0.33 (low) instead of 1.0 (very_high).
Damped score converges to the raw ratio as evidence accumulates
(5/5 ~ 0.71 high, 18/18 = 0.9 very_high). Level thresholds, readiness
mapping, and the BHV-036 single-step ladder are unchanged; on the
damped curve the ladder acts as a guard and rarely needs to clamp.

Co-Authored-By: Devin AI <158243242+devin-ai-integration[bot]@users.noreply.github.com>

commits.author: plamen.hristov@imsdevlab.onmicrosoft.com
commits.message:
Merge origin/main (squash-merged #62) into evidence-mass-damping branch
